title: PDF Page 11
content:
Updated May 2011/RDIMS 6386329 
Intro-Bio-11 
 
Aircraft Flight Manual 
 
John Hientz 
 
Education: 
 
 BSc in Mechanical Engineering - University of Saskatchewan (1975)  
 United States Air Force Test Pilot School (1978) - Flight Test Engineering 
 
TC Experience: 
 Flight Test Engineer in National Aircraft Certification from July 1987 to July 1995 and 
from October 1998 to present.   
 Domestic, lead Flight Test Engineer on BD 700 Global Express; CL 215, CL215T and 
CL415; DHC 4 Turbo Caribou and the Kelowna Flightcraft Convair 540, 580 and 5800. 
 Foreign, familiarized – Airbus A320, Boeing 737 NG, CASA CN-235, Jetstream 41, SAAB 
340, Beech 350, Beech 1900D, SA 226/227 Metroliner, Boeing Vertol BV 107, Aerospatial 
350/350B. 
 
Military Experience: 
 Maintenance Engineering at a military flight training base in Portage la Prairie Manitoba 
from 1975 to 1977. 
 Flight Test Engineer/Weapons Systems Engineer at the Canadian Forces Aerospace 
Engineering Test Establishment in Cold Lake from 1978 to 1982.  
 
Industry Experience: 
 Flight Test Engineer/Systems Engineer with Bristol Aerospace in Winnipeg from 1982 - 
1987 co-ordinating weapons clearance programs on helicopter and fixed wing aircraft.   
 Flight Test Engineer with British Aerospace in Indonesia 1997-1998, responsible for flight 
test engineering activities on the N250 and the CN 235-220 aircraft. 
 
United Nations International Civil Aviation Organization Experience: 
 Flight Test Engineering Technical Advisor to the Indonesian DGAC in Indonesia from 1995 
to 1997 in support of the civil certification of the N250 and the CN 220 aircraft. 
 Flight Test Engineering Instructor for the Indonesia DGAC.

Aging Aircraft 
 
Bogdan Gajewski 
 
Education: 
 
 1979 University of Szczecin, Poland 
 M.Sc.  Mechanical Engineering University of Szczecin, Poland 
 
TC Experience: 
 
1989 – present  Senior Corrective Action Engineer, Rotorcraft – National Aircraft  
Certification.  
                                Acted as Manager, Continuing Airworthiness, Rotorcraft. 
 
Industry Experience: 
 
1985 – 1989      Mechanical Engineer – Filtran Microcircuits Inc., Ottawa, ON 
Responsible for scheduling, design, testing, prototype fabrication and 
fixtures in machine shop and lab facilities.  
1975 – 1984  Research Engineer, Adjunct – Aviation Division, Institute of Farm  
Mechanization, Szczecin, Poland. 
Provided consultation to Aviation Institute and Agricultural Aviation 
Institute. Designed and developed measuring control devices and test 
equipment for field experiments. 
Individual fellowship in Helicopter Department of PZL-WSK Swidnik, Poland and 
International Agricultural Center, Wageningen, Holland. 
 
International Conferences: 
 
Presented two papers at AHS International Helicopter Safety Symposium, Montreal, Canada, 
September 26-29, 2005: 
 
1. Engineering Assumption versus Operational Reality: Repeated Lift Operation.  
2. Safety concerns with occupant protection where successful autorotation is not assured.

Intro-Bio-14 
Certification Flight Test with Lessons Learned from the 
Development and Flight Testing of Fire Fighting Aircraft 
 
 
Jim Martin 
 
 
Education: 
  
        Queen's University of Belfast (1969), B.Sc Aeronautical Engineering  
        Empire Test Pilots' School (1976), Graduate Flight Test Engineer 
  
TC Experience: 
  
        Joined TC in 1981 to better understand certification process 
        Opportunity to work with many different types of aircraft resulted in subsequent career as Flight Test 
Engineer in TC responsible for certification flight test engineering of aircraft performance, flight 
characteristics and systems performance.   
  
Industry Experience: 
  
        Following graduation, worked in UK a s Design Stress Engineer on  a civil aircraft project and as  a Systems 
Engineer doing simulation studies of guided weapons (British Aircraft Corporation, EASAMS) 
         Began flight testing career in 1973 as Trials Officer on military aircraft (UK Ministry of Defence) 
        Continued flight testing career with civil aircraft development and certification in the USA and 
Canada.(Canadair) 
   
Notable Achievements: 
  
         Conducted certification tests on over 60 types of aircraft
